title: Rich Mexican Chicken Enchiladas
description: A rich and flavorful Oaxacan dish featuring whole wheat tortillas and chicken in a spicy tomato sauce, perfect for a diabetic-friendly dinner option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin and paprika to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked chicken and crushed tomatoes.
In a separate pan, warm the tortillas over medium heat for about 30 seconds on each side.
To assemble the enchiladas, lay a tortilla flat and spoon some of the chicken and tomato mixture onto the center of the tortilla.
Roll the tortilla up and place seam-side down in a baking dish, repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the tortillas are lightly browned.
